prompt: Audit and improve this partially layered Flask task-management API. Target root: fixture. Save the audit to outputs/audit.md. Retain useful models, blueprints, services, and helpers; change only responsibilities that evidence shows are misplaced. Preserve routes, status semantics, response meaning, and persistence effects. Please carry the work through as far as safely possible.
assertions: Identifies Python, Flask-SQLAlchemy, the task-management domain, app.py as an entry point, SQLite persistence, and the existing layered shape.
Records boot, endpoint, task-user-category flows, persistence, and discovery limitations before making architecture judgments.
Writes outputs/audit.md with fingerprint, scope, baseline, limitations, severity summary in CRITICAL-HIGH-MEDIUM-LOW order, findings, proposed scope, approval prompt, and snapshot digest.
Contains at least five root-cause-distinct findings, including at least one CRITICAL or HIGH finding.
Every finding uses an existing repository-relative file and valid one-based inclusive line range with non-empty evidence, impact, and recommendation.
Any deprecated-API claim is tied to the detected dependency version and authoritative current documentation; otherwise deprecation uncertainty is recorded as a limitation.
Does not mutate fixture source, configuration, dependencies, schemas, databases, or tests before explicit approval tied to the reviewed report.
Retains useful models, blueprints, services, and helpers while proposing targeted responsibility corrections and complete behavioral validation.
